spec_match.rationale: The demo applies the exact path-length normalization contract to SVG path dash calculations and distinguishes the CSS property from its attribute fallback.

interactive.rationale: Shape, progress, and total controls update the path and readouts, but independent ranges permit progress to exceed the normalized total and generate an invalid negative dash gap.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature blurb promises normalized dash-driven progress without geometric measurement, and this concept provides that interaction with an honest pre-150 fallback.

edge_cases.rationale: Setting progress to 1000 and total to 100 reports 1000% and outputs stroke-dasharray: 1000 -900; the browser rejects that value and retains the previous stroke dash pattern.

references.rationale: The page links directly to ChromeStatus feature 4861677550043136 and the SVG specification pull request.

conformance.rationale: The feature conformance route reports that no suite exists, so parsing, cascade, computed value, rendering, interpolation, and zero-value contracts are not asserted.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP in Headless Chrome 149 exercised all three controls, verified readout and geometry changes, captured the invalid-range screenshot, and found successful resources/telemetry POST; DevTools also reported three form-label association issues.

telemetry_health.rationale: The shared telemetry client loaded and POSTed successfully, but the individual shape and range interactions were not exposed as inspectable interaction events during this review.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared color variables, provides an interactive stage rather than a static card, and has no local button color override risk.

accessibility.rationale: Native select and range controls have accessible names and keyboard focus, but visible labels are not programmatically associated and changing progress/readout state has no live-region semantics.

openQuestions.title: Prevent progress from exceeding the normalized total
openQuestions.detail: Couple the progress range to the selected path-length total, or model progress as a true 0–100 percentage and derive the dash value. The current total=100/progress=1000 combination emits a negative gap, which CSS rejects while the UI incorrectly reports 1000% drawn.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-level conformance suite
openQuestions.detail: Create the missing immutable suite from the SVG property contracts, covering valid and invalid parsing, initial none versus numeric zero, computed values, cascade over the presentation attribute, rendering, inheritance, and interpolation.

openQuestions.title: Associate visible labels and announce live output
openQuestions.detail: Use for/id label associations instead of relying only on aria-label, and expose the changing percentage/code/readouts through an appropriate status or live region without making slider movement excessively verbose.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP tested http://localhost:3001/v150/support-path-length-as-a-css-property/dash-progress/ in Headless Chrome 149. Shape changes (spiral to zigzag), progress, and normalized total updated the SVG, geometric length, and generated code; the unsupported CSS property produced a clear attribute fallback. The total=100/progress=1000 edge exposed a real stale-render bug because stroke-dasharray: 1000 -900 is rejected. Console had no script errors but did report three label-association issues; all network resources and the telemetry POST succeeded. /v150/support-path-length-as-a-css-property/conformance/ reported no suite. Screenshot: /tmp/showcase-v150-dash-progress-edge.png.
